summary: Large experimental migration of Dolphin `os` translation units from standalone `.s` assembly into C files, often using C with inline assembly plus separate `_data.s` files for remaining data sections. The stated goal was to make even very large TUs easier to work on incrementally: move `.text` into C first, create headers and provisional types, identify data, later migrate data and match functions. The PR removed full assembly sources such as `asm/dolphin/os/OSThread.s`, `OSReset.s`, `OSResetSW.s`, and `OSMemory.s`, added or expanded corresponding `src/dolphin/os/*.c` files, split data into files like `OSCache_data.s`, `OSContext_data.s`, `OSInterrupt_data.s`, and updated headers/build lists. Review discussion noted that much SDK OS code is handwritten assembly and therefore may be low-value or busy work for new contributors, while the author argued that placeholder `unk_t` signatures are useful because compiler errors force real signatures when symbols are used elsewhere.
what_changed: Migrated a broad set of Dolphin OS `.text` sections from assembly files into C translation units under `src/dolphin/os`.
Deleted full assembly implementations for several OS modules, notably `OSThread.s` with about 1870 deleted lines, `OSReset.s`, `OSResetSW.s`, and `OSMemory.s`.
Added large C implementations or C wrappers for OS modules, including `OSThread.c`, `OSReset.c`, `OSResetSW.c`, and `OSMemory.c`.

Split remaining data and bss content into per-module `_data.s` files such as `OSCache_data.s`, `OSContext_data.s`, `OSInterrupt_data.s`, `OSMemory_data.s`, `OSReboot_data.s`, `OSExi_data.s`, `OSRtc_data.s`, `OSSerial_data.s`, `OSThread_data.s`, and `OSTime_data.s`.
Renamed `asm/dolphin/os/OSError.s` to `asm/dolphin/os/OSError_data.s` with 100% similarity according to the diff excerpt.
Updated existing OS C files including `OS.c`, `OSSerial.c`, `OSExi.c`, `OSCache.c`, `OSContext.c`, `OSInterrupt.c`, `OSRtc.c`, `OSAlarm.c`, `OSTime.c`, `OSReboot.c`, `OSUartExi.c`, and `OSFont.c`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to reflect the migration of object files from assembly to C and the introduction of separate data assembly files.
smart_moves: Used an incremental migration workflow: first move `.text` into C, then create headers/types, then identify and migrate data, and only then clean up and match functions.
Kept unmatched code in C translation units rather than requiring a whole TU to be matched at once, making it possible to work on individual functions inside very large TUs.
Separated leftover data into `_data.s` files, preserving exact section layout and labels while allowing text code to live in C.

Leveraged the compiler as a type-pressure tool: placeholder `unk_t` was preferred over `void` because it causes compiler complaints when signatures leak elsewhere, forcing contributors to define real signatures.
Called out tool support explicitly: `dadosod` can copy assembly from C-inlined assembly, and `asm-differ` can compare C-inlined assembly versions more easily than code originating only in `.s` files.
Documented the migration workflow in the PR body so future module migrations could reuse the same staged process.
decomp_lessons: Migrating a TU to C does not require all functions to be matched immediately; C files can temporarily contain inline assembly while headers and types improve around them.
For large TUs, a partial C migration can expose call relationships, shared data types, and public interfaces more clearly than raw assembly.
Data sections can be isolated into `_data.s` files as a transitional state when text has moved to C but data has not yet been fully typed or migrated.
Using a sentinel unknown type can be more useful than defaulting to `void`, because it creates compile-time pressure to recover real function signatures.

SDK modules, especially OS code, may contain significant handwritten assembly; reviewers may consider them lower priority for new contributors because matching may be mechanical or busy work rather than high-value gameplay decomp.
A migration strategy that works for OS may be even more beneficial in gameplay systems such as `ft`, where header cleanup and extern cleanup can produce more visible decomp value.
naming_conventions: Per-module leftover data assembly files use the pattern `asm/dolphin/os/<Module>_data.s`, for example `OSCache_data.s`, `OSContext_data.s`, `OSInterrupt_data.s`, and `OSMemory_data.s`.
Dolphin OS C sources retain SDK-style names under `src/dolphin/os`, such as `OSReset.c`, `OSResetSW.c`, `OSThread.c`, `OSMemory.c`, and `OSSerial.c`.
Existing unknown labels were preserved in data files where names were not recovered, such as `lbl_80401BF0`, `lbl_80401E20`, `lbl_80402318`, and `lbl_804A7C80`.
Known OS globals were preserved with their SDK-style names where available, including `__OSLastInterruptSrr0`, `__OSLastInterrupt`, and `__OSLastInterruptTime`.
The discussion references `unk_t` as an intentional placeholder type rather than silently erasing uncertainty with `void`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move assembly `.text` into C translation units first, potentially as inline assembly, to enable per-function matching work inside the C file.
Keep data and bss in assembly `_data.s` files until types and layout are understood well enough to migrate them safely.
Use `asm-differ` against generated assembly from C-inlined assembly when matching individual functions.
Use `dadosod` or similar tooling to copy a function's inline assembly into a scratch for matching.
Preserve alignment directives, section declarations, string literals, `.4byte` entries, and `.skip` sizes in data assembly files to avoid layout drift.
When hardware or low-level SDK routines are handwritten assembly, expect that some functions may remain inline assembly or be lower priority for matching.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that many SDK OS functions are handwritten assembly and questioned the value for new contributors, saying the work may mostly remove `unk_t` and convert things to `void`; SDK had been marked low-priority for this reason.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Said there was no issue with doing the migration, but recommended not pointing new contributors at it because much of it is busy work.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that `unk_t` is a placeholder, and that using it instead of `void` is useful because compiler errors force real signatures when the type appears in places like `functions.h`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged that OS may have been a strange first candidate because much of it is handwritten assembly, but said the same approach was already helping clean up headers and externs in `ft` work.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete migration of Dolphin OS `_data.s` files into C data definitions.
Find PR #580 for the build.sh assembly dump tooling mentioned as follow-up from this migration.
Search for subsequent `ft` migration PRs by ribbanya that reused this workflow and cleaned up headers or externs.
Search for uses of `unk_t` in function declarations after this PR to see where placeholder signatures forced cleanup.
Check whether OS handwritten assembly functions remained inline assembly or were later matched in C.
